name: webFetch
description: Fetch content from a public URL. Returns raw HTML or a Markdown version.
intent: fetch content from a public URL
examples: Fetch the documentation at https://example.com/docs as Markdown
Download the raw HTML of https://example.com
Retrieve the JSON response from https://api.example.com/data
whenToUse: The content lives at a public HTTP(S) URL.
The user wants the page body, API response, or asset downloaded to local memory.
Choosing Markdown strips tags and is better for downstream LLM processing.
whenNotToUse: The content is a local file. Use readFile instead.
The content is reached via shell/network tooling outside HTTP. Use runCommand instead.
The URL is private or requires auth the tool cannot supply — the fetch will fail.

responseFormat.description: Output format. Markdown strips tags and simplifies layout which is tokens efficient.
